OUR SERVICES

YOU ARE HERE : HOME /SERVICES

We offers
Arc Flash Study And Analysis

Arc Flash Study

Care Labs is a cost-effective provider of electrical arc flash analysis, investigation, and certification services for all sorts of establishments. Arc flash threats, worker training, documentation, and PPE needs will be reviewed by trained professionals to ensure your electrical safety program is current, compliant, and complete.

Care Labs can detect arc flash concerns early on and take suitable steps to limit the effects of harmonic distortions to assure the equipment’s safety.

ETAP (Electrical Transient Analysis Program) software is used for Arc flash analysis and investigation. An arc flash is the amount of light and heat produced as a result of an arc fault. This occurs when current travels via an air gap between the conductors. 

The arc flash ionizes the air and emits a great deal of light and heat (approximately 35,000 degrees Fahrenheit). An arc flash analysis is the process of researching and analysing a facility’s electrical installations to estimate the amount of incident energy available for each electrical equipment. 

Arc flash studies promote safe working conditions by establishing safety guidelines for qualified electricians to work on equipment and circuits. A short circuit and coordination analysis, as well as updated electrical one-line schematics and equipment locations, are also included.

Arc Flash Study Objectives:

  • To determine the level of harm caused by incident energy emitted during an arc flash fault. 
  • Changing the settings of protective devices to limit incident energy exposure 
  • Make safety advice for arc flash hazards. 
  • Follow the norms and regulations unique to your country. 
  • To ascertain the sort of PPE worn while working. 

The Negative Effects of Arc Flash: 

  • Arcs cause serious burns to the skin, face, and eyes.
  • Inhaling gases and hot particles can result in serious internal burns to the throat and lungs. 
  • Flying debris and loosened components injuries 

Arc flash studies are crucial for reducing dangers and increasing safety for electrical staff, as well as the firm’s overall safety. Arc flash studies aid in the determination of electrical danger levels and the implementation of appropriate safety measures to reduce the risk of burns and accidents. All utilities, manufacturing, industrial, and commercial companies that employ electrical disconnects, motor control, panel boards, and switchboards need arc flash analysis.

Arc Flash Research’s Advantages: 

  • improved human and machine safety
  • Reduced medical and legal costs 
  • Regulatory compliance with safety requirements 
  • Reduced standards infractions and fines 

For arc flash analysis, the following steps are taken: 

  • Data gathering 
  • Performing field verification 
  • Design of a system   
  • Examination of arc flashes 

Care Labs Work Process Arc Flash Study

Care Labs provides outstanding services and will supply any services required by the site crew, local contractor, or engineering firm. 

Our work procedure consists of the following steps: 

  •  Data gathering 
  •  Modeling a power system with ETAP 
  •  Short-circuit investigation 
  •  A study on the coordination of overcurrent protective devices 
  •  An inquiry into arc flashes 
  •  Testing for arc flashes 
  •  Keeping records 
  •  Methods for reducing the risk of arc flash 
  •  Full-size one-line diagrams 
  •  Installing arc flash labelling 
  •  The completed report 

Advantages of Using Care Labs Services: 

  • Create more secure electricity systems that follow standards and laws. 
  • Complete integrated short circuit, overcurrent coordination, device evaluation, and arc flash evaluation modules are integrated with protective devices and bus ratings. 
  •  Creating a safer working environment 
  •  Quick and simple alternative proposal for optimal design 
  • Increased safety margins through arc fault tolerances that are user-definable 
  • Save time by generating arc flash labels automatically with our computer-aided analysis. 
  •  Reduce lost productivity and maintenance expenditures. 

Crew members are placed in various areas within care labs, guaranteeing that our specialists are nearby in the event of a routine or emergency. Care Labs has quickly established itself as an ISO 9001:2008 accredited firm, with a renowned client base that has provided excellent feedback. We provide arc flash study and analysis services in all major cities, including Athens, Thessaloniki, Patras, Larissa, and Heraklion. 

Contact us immediately for a free arc flash research and analysis quote.

title: Harmonic Study & Analysis
metaDescription: Conduct harmonic study and analysis in Greece with Carelabs. Improve energy efficiency, ensure power quality, and protect critical industrial equipment.

OUR SERVICES

YOU ARE HERE : HOME /SERVICES

We offers
Harmonic Study & Analysis

Greece's Harmonic Study & Analysis

Care Labs provides electrical installation services such as electrical safety inspections, testing, calibration, and certification, as well as full harmonic investigation and analysis. Trained professionals will review harmonic risks, training programs, documentation, and personal protective equipment (PPE) to ensure your electrical safety program is current, compliant, and comprehensive. 

Care Labs can detect harmonics early on and take suitable steps to limit the impact of harmonic distortions in order to assure the equipment’s safety. Electric voltages and currents known as harmonics can cause inefficiencies in the electrical system. They generate inefficient to heat and can degrade power quality. 

Harmonic voltage analysis is a mathematical method for predicting potential resonances and harmonic distortion levels based on data. Nonlinear electrical switching devices such as AC variable frequency drives, DC drives, fluorescent lights, computers, and so on introduce harmonic frequencies into the electrical system. 

During high harmonic voltage/current levels, there is a high risk of equipment malfunction. As a result, a timely harmonic study and analysis are critical. Harmonic limits in 415 voltage systems are currently 5%, with 4% odd harmonics and 2% even harmonics. 

Harmonic Distortion Effects: 

  • Is responsible for circulating current and high voltage. 
  • Equipment failure and destruction as a result of excessive voltage distortion 
  • An increase in the presence of internal energy in components, resulting in component failure and a shorter life span. 
  • Tripping of branch circuit breakers due to a fault 
  • Measuring error 
  • Fires and explosions in the wiring and distribution system 
  • Generator outages 
  • A decrease in the system power factor 
  • Expensive installation and utility fees 
  • Productivity losses 
  • Downtime and unnecessary drive tripping 
  • The use of larger motors 

Harmonic problems are extremely unlikely to occur, but when they do occur, they tend to reduce power system reliability, power quality, and efficiency. As a result, harmonic study and analysis are essential. 

  • Protects against voltage and current harmonics 
  • Restriction on frequency variation 
  • Unbalanced voltage and current 
  • Improve power factor analysis and keep the value near one. 
  • Regulates voltage fluctuations 

Harmonic study and analysis will provide insight into the potential cause and effect of power quality issues, assisting in the avoidance of harmonic-related issues during the design stage and reducing the possibility of undesired effects. 

Harmonic Study and Analysis Work Process at Care Labs: 

  • From the electrical one-line diagram, highlight available nonlinear loads, capacitor banks, and medium voltage cables of long length. 
  • Locate the point of common coupling (PCC) 
  • Identify in-plant system buses that are susceptible to harmonic distortions. 
  • Collect harmonics data from all nonlinear loads. 
  • Obtain critical data on voltage and current harmonics at the predetermined PCC, including the highest and lowest short circuit fault levels.
  • Using ETAP, conduct harmonic analysis on the electrical network. 
  • Determine the total and discrete levels of current and voltage wave distortions at the buses and PCC. 
  • Draw the harmonic frequency spectrum. 
  • If the harmonic distortion levels exceed the allowable levels, an appropriate mitigation solution is proposed and implemented. 
  • Rerun the analysis after incorporating the mitigation solution. 

Benefits of Care Labs Service:

If the background harmonics have already been measured, stage 2 involves higher ratings.
  • If the background is less than 25%, stage 2 allows for variable converter size based on fault level and converter type. 
  • If the background is between 25% and 75%, converter ratings of half the value given above are permitted. 
  • A full harmonic investigation is performed for higher background. 
Care Labs has crew members deployed in various locations, ensuring that our specialists are nearby in the event of a routine or emergency. Care Labs has quickly established itself as an ISO 9001:2008 accredited firm, with a solid client base that has provided excellent feedback.We provide harmonic research and analysis services in Athens, Thessaloniki, Patras, Larissa, and Heraklion, to name a few significant cities in Greece. Contact us today to book your harmonic study and analysis in Greece or to get a quote!

OUR SERVICES

YOU ARE HERE : HOME /SERVICES

We offers
Motor Start Analysis

Motor Start Analysis in Greece

Care Labs provides a specialized Motor start study and analysis service to businesses throughout Greece. The primary goal of our motor start analysis service is to evaluate the unusual consequences of starting a large motor. Aside from successfully connecting the electric motor to the line voltage, there are several factors to consider before starting an electric motor, such as nuisance tripping, excessive nominal currents, and flickering or dimming of light, all of which indicate that a power system isn’t functioning properly. 

Care Labs will send trained experts to your location to collect current and historical motor installation data. Then, using the obtained data and input from the manufacturer, calculations are performed to forecast future consequences, and solutions to minimize the effects are proposed. 

Both static and dynamic simulation approaches can be used to analyze motor starting techniques. We primarily focus on dynamic motor starting studies that simulate motor starting conditions such as:

  • The observed starting impedance of the electric motor is modelled to obtain the load flow solution. 
  • For pre-fault short circuit situations, short-circuit method calculations are used, and voltage drop calculations are used for motor starting currents.

  • When a precise dynamic model of the electric motor circuit and load-torque characteristics exist, the dynamic model of the electric motor can be used in a standard transient stability algorithm to calculate the effect of motor starting. 
  • When comprehensive dynamic model information for the electric motor is unavailable, transient stability simulations can be used. 
  • When a precise dynamic model of the electric motor circuit and load-torque characteristics exist, the dynamic model of the electric motor can be used in a standard transient stability algorithm to calculate the effect of motor starting. 
  • When comprehensive dynamic model information for the electric motor is unavailable, transient stability simulations can be used. 

Motor Start Analysis is Required in Greece: 

The use of motors in modern industrial systems is increasing at an exponential rate, as is the size of the electric motor. 

Starting a large line-connected motor can disrupt the electric motor, locally linked loads, and electrically distant buses.

When starting an electric motor, the voltage at the motor terminal should be kept at 80% or higher of the rated voltage. Voltage difference affects a motor’s load torque because the square of its terminal voltage is exactly proportional to it.

If the voltage drop is substantial, operating machines may exceed their breakdown torque and decelerate or stall. Voltage drop affects electrical equipment, sensitive control devices, lighting loads, and more.

Furthermore, excessive starting current reduces terminal voltage, which increases the current drawn by all systems serving a continuous load. Under voltage relays are also found to be malfunctioning.

When to Have a Motor Start Analysis: 

  • Before or during the purchase of a large motor. 
  • If no generator is present and the motor rating exceeds 30% of the base KVA rating of the supply transformer. 
  • If the system is fed solely by the generator and the motor rating exceeds 10-15% of the KVA rating of the generator. 
  • A motor start study may be required if a large number of motors start at the same time for a process in an industry. 
  • Because smaller power systems typically have limited capacity, voltage drop issues when starting a high-capacity motor are magnified. Analysis of motor starting aids in understanding the output of small systems containing generators. 

Advantages of Motor Start Analysis: 

Motor start-up analysis Selecting a motor or starting technique with optimal speed-torque characteristics is advantageous. To avoid exceeding the torque limits of the equipment, any special loads must be accelerated under closely monitored conditions. It is possible to analyze the shaft operation of the motor at any time by performing a motor start analysis. As a result, it aids in determining the permissible torque limits of the equipment. 

Motor starting studies establish machine voltage and if the electric motor can start and accelerate. To further assess your electric motor, Care Labs offers motor start studies by trained specialists.

We offer motor start analysis services in all major cities, including Athens, Thessaloniki, Patras, Larissa, and Heraklion. Book your motor starting study today or contact us for a quote!

Motor Start Analysis is Required in Greece: 

The use of motors in modern industrial systems is increasing at an exponential rate, as is the size of the electric motor. 

Starting a large line-connected motor can disrupt the electric motor, locally linked loads, and electrically distant buses.

When starting an electric motor, the voltage at the motor terminal should be kept at 80% or higher of the rated voltage. Voltage difference affects a motor’s load torque because the square of its terminal voltage is exactly proportional to it.

If the voltage drop is substantial, operating machines may exceed their breakdown torque and decelerate or stall. Voltage drop affects electrical equipment, sensitive control devices, lighting loads, and more.

Furthermore, excessive starting current reduces terminal voltage, which increases the current drawn by all systems serving a continuous load. Under voltage relays are also found to be malfunctioning.

When to Have a Motor Start Analysis: 
Before or during the purchase of a large motor. 
If no generator is present and the motor rating exceeds 30% of the base KVA rating of the supply transformer. 
If the system is fed solely by the generator and the motor rating exceeds 10-15% of the KVA rating of the generator. 
A motor start study may be required if a large number of motors start at the same time for a process in an industry. 
Because smaller power systems typically have limited capacity, voltage drop issues when starting a high-capacity motor are magnified. Analysis of motor starting aids in understanding the output of small systems containing generators. 
Advantages of Motor Start Analysis: 

Motor start-up analysis Selecting a motor or starting technique with optimal speed-torque characteristics is advantageous. To avoid exceeding the torque limits of the equipment, any special loads must be accelerated under closely monitored conditions. It is possible to analyze the shaft operation of the motor at any time by performing a motor start analysis. As a result, it aids in determining the permissible torque limits of the equipment. 

Motor starting studies establish machine voltage and if the electric motor can start and accelerate. To further assess your electric motor, Care Labs offers motor start studies by trained specialists.

We offer motor start analysis services in all major cities, including Athens, Thessaloniki, Patras, Larissa, and Heraklion. Book your motor starting study today or contact us for a quote!

OUR SERVICES

YOU ARE HERE : HOME /SERVICES

We offers
Power Quality Analysis

Analysis of Power Quality in Greece

Care Labs analyses power quality for businesses all over Greece. Our goal is to protect your electrical installations, electrical equipment, and appliances from any problems caused by power supply fluctuations. 

Many businesses, as well as an increasing number of private customers, now use data collection, monitoring, and instrumentation equipment that is highly susceptible to power supply fluctuations. While most electrical products are designed to withstand certain supply variations, very few are designed to withstand the voltage fluctuation spectrum that is a common feature of distributed electricity. 

Professional experts from Care Labs will help you resolve power quality issues in the areas of equipment installation, control, protection, and power conditioning.

Power Quality Analysis's Goal: 

  • The assessment of inconsistencies in the electrical system and the load. 
  • Evaluating the work environment in order to improve simulation methods for providing power quality solutions. 
  • Predicting the operation and efficiency of load equipment 
  • Increase the power factor and reliability of the machine. 
  • Avoid excessive energy consumption. 
  • To avoid breakdowns and disruptions. 
  • Reducing power quality anomalies like harmonics, spikes, and transients. 
  • Aid in the prevention of voltage/frequency surges and PLC hang-ups. 
  • To avoid, among other things, transformer overheating, capacitor explosion, and nuisance tripping. 
  • Significantly reduce utility bills 
  • Extend the life of the electrical network and its components. 
  • Examine electrical equipment to ensure that it can withstand variations. 

Power Quality Analysis is Required in Greece: 

All electrical networks experience supply interruptions, which are more common when power lines are damaged by storms, natural disasters, or accidents. Power supply fluctuations are completely normal. Fluctuations, disturbances, and distortions in the characteristics of power supplied by an electricity network fall into three categories: 

1. Frequency deviations occur when a frequency exceeds its natural frequency range. 

2. Voltage disturbances occur when the voltage shape is preserved but the voltage amplitude exceeds the normal range. 

2.1 Voltage sag (dip) and voltage swells of up to one minute duration are examples of short-duration variations. 

2.2 Long-duration fluctuations include under voltages and over voltages that last more than one minute. 

2.3 Unbalanced voltage – occurs when the load is unevenly distributed across the phases, causing significant heating of the equipment. 

2.4  Voltage differences between neutral and earth 

3. Disturbances caused by distortion – when the voltage shape is not uniform or sinusoidal. 

3.1  Waveform distortions: Harmonics and inter-harmonics cause device heating, increased losses, inefficient power consumption, and resonance problems. 

3.2  Transients that last less than one-tenth of a second, but are capable of degrading switch contact surfaces, inducing inefficiency in the system, and leading to premature failure of motor drives and control.  

What Is Included in Power Quality Analysis? 

Different approaches are required to identify various power quality disturbances; a few of these approaches are: 

  • Harmonic Analysis: The use of computer algorithms to identify and predict potential harmonic problems, as well as reduction techniques, is involved in harmonic analysis. 
  • Surge and Transient Analysis: This study will identify transients and surges. 
  • Voltage Dips and Swells Analysis: This analysis would track the short-term drop and rise in voltage, as well as its value and path. 
  • Analysis of Reactive Power The preferred reactive power at the distribution and load ends will be determined by this analysis. 
  • Captive Power Analysis: This research would quantify and synchronize captive power in order to meet demand while lowering energy surcharges and fuel consumption. 
  • Load Flow Analysis: This study forecasts power flow magnitudes, power factors, voltage levels, and losses in system branches based on the defined operating conditions. 

Care Labs is dedicated to providing the best service possible to improve the quality and dependability of your electrical installation for the benefit of all its customers. 

In all major cities, including Athens, Thessaloniki, Patras, Larissa, and Heraklion, we offer power quality analysis services. Contact us today to schedule your power quality analysis or to request a quote!

OUR SERVICES

YOU ARE HERE : HOME /SERVICES

We offers
Power System Study & Analysis

Power System Research and Analysis in Greece :

Care Labs offers a variety of electrical installation services, including electrical safety inspections, electrical system design simulations, power quality analysis studies, and power system study and analysis. 

Our team of electrical professionals uses ETAP, the best power system transient analysis tool, to perform load flow and short circuit studies, protective device coordination studies, harmonic emission, and filter design studies, transient analysis, and network stability studies. 

It allows us to collaborate closely with clients in order to design and deliver the best power compensation and solutions available. 

Why Is a Power System Analysis Study Necessary?

Every industrial or commercial facility relies on a consistent, dependable, and well-maintained power system. Even a minor power outage or failure can have disastrous consequences, such as extended downtime, damage to critical equipment, and its repair. A periodic power system analysis study on your facility’s power system could ensure that it is safe, efficient, and reliable in both normal and abnormal conditions. At every step of designing, setting up, and running a power system, it must carefully analyze.

Our power system studies and analyses will provide an overview of how the system will respond under normal operating and transient fault conditions using the ETAP software tool. It defines a collection of various engineering analysis investigations used to analyze an electric power system’s response to events over various time periods examined in a power.

Many different studies reflect the status of your facility’s power system. It can be included in a power system analysis study report; some examples are: 

  • Circuit breaker 
  • Coordination of security measures 
  • Flow of Load 
  • Dynamic and transient evaluation 
  • Arc Flash Investigation 
  • Power Efficiency 
  • Analysis of harmonics 
  • Starting the motor 
  • Earthing research 

It enables industries to establish a baseline for other aspects of overall plant operation, particularly the electrical safety program. These studies can demonstrate potentially safe and dependable operations. 

Load Flow Analysis: 

Load flow studies enable the calculation of voltages and currents in various parts of the network, as well as the selection of appropriately rated equipment. 

ETAP’s built-in load flow function can be used to perform load flows, which provide information on power loading and power factor, as well as voltages and currents in a power system.

Short-Circuit Research: 

A power system needs short-circuit studies to determine how a fault conditions occur. Carrying out short circuit studies by hand is a time-consuming process, and it is far more efficient to use capable software such as ETAP.

ETAP is used to model system protective devices and evaluate the impact of 3-phase, L-G, L-L, and L-L-G faults in distribution systems. 

Transient Research: 

Transient studies compute the magnitude and shapes of transient overvoltage and currents caused by lightning strikes and switching operations. The results determine the amount of insulation and surge protection required.

Protection Coordination Research: 

This study is used to validate the various security devices in your system. It checks to see if they are properly coordinated and sized for the equipment they are protecting. 

 

The following key functions are generally included in our power system analysis study process: 

  • Data collection procedure 
  • Data analysis and client meeting 
  • Gathering equipment data 
  • Model of a single-line diagram 
  • ETAP power system modeling 
  • Short circuit and load flow analysis 
  • Research on protection coordination 
  • Arc flash analysis 
  • An overview of the study report 
  • Risk evaluation and mitigation analysis 
  • Project administration 

Care Labs Power System Analysis consists of the following components: 

  • Possibilities for lowering energy demand and costs. 
  • Reports that highlight problem areas. 
  • Suggestions for solutions 
  • Information about how and where energy is consumed. 
  • Confirmation of protective device ratings. 
  • Assist in avoiding damage and injuries. 
  • Harmonics and transients’ effects 

Our power system analysis studies focus on assessing the client’s electrical system and making recommendations to improve its performance while increasing supply security. 

Athens, Thessaloniki, Patras, Larissa, and Heraklion are just a few of the major cities where we offer power system analysis and study services. Contact us today to schedule your power system study and analysis or to request a quote!
